ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] (Plutarch: Beginner Track) [This article and video](https://greekreporter.com/2024/06/09/plutarch-biographer-alexander-the-great-greeks/) give some background on Plutarch, the biographer of Publicola. 
[ ] (Plutarch: Beginner Track) Explore [this timeline](https://www.heritage-history.com/index.php?c=resources&s=study-cat&h=ancient_rome&f=timeline) of Ancient Rome.

[ ] (Plutarch: Beginner Track) View [maps](https://www.heritage-history.com/index.php?c=resources&s=study-page&h=ancient_rome&f=historical_maps) of some Roman cities.
[ ] (Plutarch: Beginner Track) Watch [this video](https://youtu.be/_Wl6IJl2AHM?si=7ptzitfx2qNqzUJi) about the Temple of Jupiter.

[ ] (Plutarch: Beginner Track) If Plutarch were alive today, what political or military leaders do you think he would write about? Write a modern version of Plutarch's Lives featuring one (or comparing two) of these modern leaders. 
[ ] (Plutarch: Beginner Track) Explore the Plutarch Podcast and other resources available on [this website](https://grammaticus.co/resources-for-reading-plutarch/). (Teachers should preview all content.)
